note: Solid circles reported by RNZ; outlined circles link to party websites. Full party-site sweep completed 22 Jul 2026. Enriched 24 Jul 2026: descriptions, Political (econ/social 1-5, null = no lean), why, confidence per Policy Intake prompt v3.

policies.title: A Home for Everybody — 40k homes, rent caps
policies.description: Legislates a 2 percent annual cap on rent increases, scraps no-cause evictions, introduces a rental Warrant of Fitness and landlord register, and expands public housing by tens of thousands of homes.

policies.title: Medicard — 3 free GP visits
policies.description: Gives every New Zealander a Medicard from birth or residency carrying three free GP visits a year from July 2028, backed by an independent GP pricing authority; funded by the capital gains tax.

policies.title: Free prescriptions, scans, screening
policies.description: Removes the $5 prescription fee from 1 July 2027 and adds free maternity scans (July 2028, $28.6m a year) and free cervical screening for ages 25-69 (October 2027) to the Medicard.

policies.title: Kaupapa Māori mental health investment
policies.description: Investment in kaupapa Maori mental health services alongside community-led alternatives to the prison system.
policies.confidence_note: Cited article's retrieved text covers the justice policy; the mental-health investment detail needs verifying against the full piece

policies.title: NZ Future Fund — SOE dividends into NZ investment.
policies.description: Establishes a New Zealand-only investment fund seeded with dividends from selected state-owned assets plus a one-off $200m Crown injection, with seeded assets protected from sale in legislation.
policies.confidence_note: Party withholding asset list and fund scale until in government

policies.title: Expand Apprenticeship Boost
policies.description: Restores the $500-a-month employer subsidy to two years from 2028, adds road construction, water treatment and hairdressing from 2027, with $1,000 tool grants and funded mentoring - about $56.5m a year.

policies.title: Productivity Unleashed — R&D 2% GDP, AI rules
policies.description: Lifts R&D spending to at least 2 percent of GDP, builds a regulatory framework for AI, forgives student loan interest, and opens regulated sectors including cannabis and gene technology.
policies.confidence_note: Explainer-level source; site now brands this area Breakthrough Economy - retitle question stands

policies.title: $20/week public transport fare cap
policies.description: Caps weekly public transport fares at $20 in Auckland, Wellington and Christchurch and $10 everywhere else from 1 July 2027, costing about $65m a year from the National Land Transport Fund.

policies.title: SolarSaver + $3k solar grants
policies.description: A $160m four-year package: up to $3,000 solar subsidies for low and middle income households, two Crown-backed loan schemes repaid via rates or power bills, and a $30m community battery fund, running within 12 months.
policies.confidence_note: Size sits on the Niche/Significant boundary - own press conference and $160m, but constituency-targeted; held at Niche

policies.title: Winter Energy Payment for students
policies.description: Member's bill extending the Winter Energy Payment to full-time tertiary students and temporarily raising it by $50 a week to match the fuel crisis relief payment.

policies.title: Home Energy Fund — solar and battery loans
policies.description: A central-local government entity ($7m Crown equity for 20 percent) offering low-interest loans secured against property and repaid through rates, plus solar installation without council consent.

policies.title: Rein in monopolies — banks, supermarkets
policies.description: Strengthens competition enforcement against monopoly power in banking, supermarkets and building materials.
policies.confidence_note: Explainer-level source

policies.title: 28% targeted CGT on property
policies.description: A 28 percent tax on profits from selling property other than the family home and farms, from July 2027, not retrospective, raising about $700m a year for the Medicard.

policies.title: Compulsory KiwiSaver + Baby Boost
policies.description: Makes KiwiSaver compulsory for all workers from 1 July 2028, lifts contributions to 6 percent each by 2032, auto-enrols babies from July 2027 with a $1,500 Baby Boost, and adds parental-leave contributions - over $1b across four years.
policies.confidence_note: Flagship pull noted; held Significant for like-for-like with policy-019

policies.title: Wealth, corporate + inheritance taxes
policies.description: A 2.5 percent annual tax on net assets over $10m, a 33 percent tax on inheritances and gifts over $1m, and higher corporate and bank taxes, funding a $10,000 tax-free threshold and a 45 percent rate over $160,000.

policies.title: Shrink govt — 43 depts to 19
policies.description: Consolidates 43 government departments into 19 and cuts ministers from 28 to 18, with single-minister accountability, fixed-term removable chief executives, and the Public Service Commission scrapped.

policies.title: Offshore oil and gas surveys
policies.description: Invests an initial $1b in a National Subsurface Development Survey of offshore basins covering oil, gas, geothermal and CO2 storage, with top basins surveyed within two years and a royalty above 50 percent as production matures.
policies.confidence_note: Mechanism flagged: a state survey programme reads closer to 4 than the recorded 5

policies.title: Boost Kiwibank by buying BNZ
policies.description: Buys back BNZ from National Australia Bank and merges it with Kiwibank into a fully Crown-owned National Bank of New Zealand, funded by sovereign bonds, Crown debt, Future Fund and ACC investment.

policies.title: Compulsory KiwiSaver, 10% contributions
policies.description: Makes KiwiSaver compulsory across the workforce with employee and employer contributions eventually rising to 10 percent, offset by unspecified tax cuts.
policies.confidence_note: No dates or costings given; 'eventually' is the only timing

policies.title: Abundant Energy — 30GW by 2050
policies.description: Seeks cross-party agreement to add 30GW of renewable generation by 2050, ring-fences Crown power profits for renewables, merges energy regulators and consolidates 29 lines companies to no more than eight.

policies.title: Abolish prisons by 2040
policies.description: Phases out prisons by 2040 in favour of community-led justice built on accountability, healing trauma and reconnecting whakapapa; the party could not supply implementation detail when asked.
policies.confidence_note: Party unable to provide mechanics to the Herald; scored on stated design

policies.title: Sexual offenders sentencing
policies.description: Prohibits judges from treating good character as a mitigating factor at sentencing for all sexual offending, building on the 40 percent cap on sentencing discounts.

policies.title: Five-policy crime package
policies.description: Accredited security guards with detention and exclusion powers, three-strikes burglary minimums, deportation liability for serious-offence resident visa holders, 100,000 rural patrol hours, and a coercion-through-animal-abuse offence.

policies.title: Sentencing Act reform pledge
policies.description: Pledges to reform the Sentencing Act next term to deliver mandatory minimum prison sentences, including for assaults on first responders, after opposing their absence from the Crimes Amendment Bill.
policies.confidence_note: Source found (NZ Herald) - closes the v001 unsourced flag; verified left false pending the RNZ-only convention call

policies.title: Healthy Oceans — end bottom trawling, 30% marine reserves
policies.description: Moves fisheries from single-species quotas to ecosystem-based management, phases out bottom trawling and expands marine protection toward 30 percent of the ocean.

policies.title: Tax Reset — land tax, Citizen Income, super means-testing
policies.description: A 1.75 percent urban and 0.5 percent rural land value tax funding a $19,400-a-year Citizen's Income for over-18s replacing main benefits, plus compulsory KiwiSaver 2.0 and three income brackets from 28 to 39 percent.

policies.title: Citizens' Voice — democratic participation reform
policies.description: Citizen assemblies matching Parliament in size to consider and advise on major issues, overseen by an independent Parliamentary Commissioner.
policies.confidence_note: Explainer-level source

policies.title: Affordable Housing — land value tax, house prices must fall
policies.description: Uses the Land Value Tax to deliberately bring house prices down while attacking an 80,000-home shortage through simplified national zoning, streamlined small-build consents and underwriting affordable developments.

policies.title: Smart On Crime
policies.description: Decriminalises drug possession but not supply, expands drug and mental-health court pilots, and redirects prison spending toward programmes proven to reduce crime.
policies.confidence_note: Explainer-level source

policies.title: Future-fit Education
policies.description: Overhauls early childhood education with centre size limits, tighter ratios, pay parity with school teachers and support for parent-led centres, plus civics from Year 6, reduced classroom screen time and funded night schools.

policies.title: Healthy People — primary care focus, reinstate Smokefree 2025
policies.description: Refocuses health spending on prevention and primary care, lifts pay and conditions for health workers, and reinstates the Smokefree 2025 legislation.
policies.confidence_note: Site page is directional; detailed policy unreleased

policies.title: Climate Action
policies.description: Adds an agricultural emissions mechanism to the ETS, excludes new exotic forestry, hands budget-setting to the Climate Change Commission, and rewards soil and wetland carbon sequestration.
policies.confidence_note: Mechanism flagged: ETS amendment reads closer to 4 than the recorded 5

policies.title: Healthy Land
policies.description: Increases conservation and biosecurity funding focused on wetlands and native reforestation, tightens water-quality regulation with independent oversight, and builds nature markets rewarding biodiversity.

policies.title: Intergenerational Infrastructure
policies.description: A 30-year infrastructure framework set apolitically by the Infrastructure Commission, significantly higher public investment, free public transport for all users, completed RMA replacement and mandated asset-management standards.
policies.confidence_note: Free-all-public-transport alone gives this a Flagship pull; held Significant

policies.title: Clean Up Politics
policies.description: Limits and discloses political donations, regulates professional lobbyists, and establishes an Anti-Corruption Commission with enforcement powers.
policies.confidence_note: Explainer-level source; neither axis engaged by the mechanism

policies.title: Honouring Te Tiriti
policies.description: Repeals the Treaty Principles Bill and Takutai Moana Amendment if passed, embeds Aotearoa history including Te Tiriti at all education levels, and grows te reo teaching capacity.
policies.confidence_note: Site page is directional

policies.title: Drink Swim Fish — freshwater and oceans plan
policies.description: Bans bottom trawling, lowers the nitrate limit for drinking water, and sets swimmable-rivers and ocean-protection rules across eight policy interventions.

policies.title: Automatic union membership for new employees
policies.description: Amends the Employment Relations Act so new employees in workplaces covered by a collective agreement are automatically union members, with the right to opt out.

policies.title: Family Doctor Loan Scheme
policies.description: A loan scheme from 1 July 2027 helping GPs buy into local practices and stay in their communities.

policies.title: Defending equal rights — Treaty principles campaign
policies.description: Continues the campaign to define Treaty principles in law based on the 1840 texts - the same rights and duties for all citizens - alongside opposing co-governance arrangements in public bodies.

policies.title: Hunting and firearms reform
policies.description: Rewrites the Arms Act winding back the firearms registry, keeps operating relief for clubs and ranges, and gives hunters a formal role in game-animal and pest management.
policies.confidence_note: Social axis strains here: the autonomy-versus-authority definition scores gun liberalisation Progressive - flagged for the methodology page

policies.title: End the supermarket duopoly
policies.description: Legislates to break Foodstuffs into two competing cooperatives (New World/Four Square and Pak'nSave), lifts Commerce Commission penalties to Australian levels, and gives the Grocery Commissioner binding powers.

policies.title: Disestablish Auckland's Independent Māori Statutory Board
policies.description: A member's bill disestablishing Auckland's Independent Maori Statutory Board (Houkura), transferring its functions, property and obligations back to Auckland Council.

policies.title: Commonwealth Games bid for Christchurch
policies.description: insufficient source detail
policies.confidence_note: Generic party news page cited; no policy-specific announcement located. Existing display values retained pending a real source. Recorded Mechanism 5 also looks wrong for a games bid.

policies.title: Next Billion Customers - trade expansion strategy
policies.description: Plan to pursue new trade agreements across Europe, South America, Asia and Africa - markets totalling almost one billion potential customers - with reduced trade barriers, streamlined border processes and digital export tools, toward doubling export value by 2034.
policies.confidence_note: Page is aspirational - no fiscal commitment or named agreements; 2034 doubling goal is pre-existing government policy

policies.title: Faster commercial building consents
policies.description: Formalises producer statements in the Building Act so qualified engineers can sign off certain commercial building work without council inspection, and designates a specialist national Building Consent Authority for large or complex commercial buildings.

policies.title: Consent must be a yes - Crimes Act change
policies.description: Amends the Crimes Act to define sexual consent as actively given - a communicated yes by words or actions rather than the absence of a no - without shifting the burden of proof in prosecutions.
